issues.description: Unrestricted file operations in save-context handler - no validation of file paths before writing to work tracking files
issues.recommendation: Add path validation to ensure files are written only within allowed directories (work-tracking/active/*)

issues.description: Command execution without validation in tool integration - commands could be injected through workflow context
issues.recommendation: Implement command whitelist and input sanitization before tool execution

issues.description: Unvalidated user input in handler triggers - malicious input could be processed without validation
issues.recommendation: Add input validation and sanitization for all trigger patterns

issues.description: Missing error handling in cross-system integration handlers - failed operations could leak information
issues.recommendation: Add comprehensive error handling with safe fallbacks

issues.description: Potential information disclosure in failure modes - error messages could reveal system internals
issues.recommendation: Sanitize error messages to remove sensitive details

issues.description: No rate limiting on handler execution - potential for DoS through rapid invocation
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting on handler execution

issues.description: No audit logging for handler execution - security incidents difficult to trace
issues.recommendation: Add audit logging for all handler executions

issues.description: Missing input length limits - potential memory exhaustion through large inputs

summary: BUILDING-BETTER.md contains several security vulnerabilities, primarily around file operations and command execution. The most critical issues involve path traversal in save-context handler and potential command injection in workflow-to-tool. While these are template files, implementing handlers without proper security controls could lead to serious vulnerabilities.
